description: regenerate a chip's per-skill indexes + manifest under canonical hashing; record the old->new chip_sha transition
rules: operate on TARGET_CHIP — point it at a chip copy, never re-pin the live chip in place by accident
observe drift against the COMMITTED index BEFORE regenerating — pre_drift is the falsifiable signal
the transition (old_chip_sha -> new_chip_sha) is the SV-1 evidence — record it
usage: Set TARGET_CHIP (a chip dir: skill dirs each with a perks.json). Output: repin.json with {pre_drift, old_chip_sha, new_chip_sha, status}. Exit nonzero iff the committed pins were stale.
limitation: Canonical re-pinning under the current sha256 scheme. The cross-language golden-vector replay (the vectors perk) and the independent Go verifier diff (the crosslang perk) arrive with infra/cwp + the Go verifier (plan P0).
summary: observe drift vs committed pins, regenerate indexes + manifest, record the chip_sha transition
